name: Ayllón - San Francisco Convent Circular Trail
name_original: Convento de San Francisco desde Ayllón — circular
description: Explore history and nature on the moderate 4.9-mile (7.9 km) circular hike from Ayllón, visiting the Main Square and San Francisco Convent.
region_intro: Hiking around Ayllón offers access to a diverse landscape characterized by the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de Ayllón, dramatic river gorges, and ancient forests. The region features impressive canyons carved by rivers like the Riaza and Duratón, alongside significant beech forests such as Hayedo de Tejera Negra. This varied geography provides a range of elevations and natural features, making it suitable for different hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Ayllón The most popular hiking route is Trail of the Red and Black Villages I, a 7.1 miles (11.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 33 minutes to complete, connecting traditional villages with distinctive architecture. Another top favourite among local hikers is Trail of the Red and Black Villages II, a moderate 9.9 miles (15.9 km) path that extends through more of the region's unique red and black villages. Local hikers also love the Nacimiento Río Aguisejo — circular por Grado del Pico, a 2.6 miles (4.3 km) trail leading through river landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 8 minutes. Hiking around Ayllón is defined by river gorges, ancient beech forests, and the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de Ayllón. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy riverside walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Ayllón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Ayllón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails are available around Ayllón?
A: Ayllón offers a diverse range of hiking trails, from easy riverside walks to more challenging mountain ascents. The region is characterized by the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de Ayllón, dramatic river gorges like those of the Riaza and Duratón, and ancient forests such as the Hayedo de Tejera Negra. There are over 40 routes available, catering to various skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, Ayllón has several easy trails. For a pleasant riverside walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1155037299/nacimiento-rio-aguisejo-circular-por-grado-del-pico" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nacimiento Río Aguisejo — circular por Grado del Pico&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 4.3 km long and takes just over an hour to complete. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1156884664/ayllon-san-francisco-convent-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ayllón - San Francisco Convent Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7.8 km route with minimal elevation gain.

Q: Where can I find challenging hikes around Ayllón?
A: For more challenging hikes, the region offers routes with greater elevation gain and longer distances. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1315673019/martin-munoz-de-ayllon-becerril-y-madriguera-circular-desde-villacorta-pueblos-rojos-y-negros-de-segovia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Martín Muñoz de Ayllón, Becerril y Madriguera — circular desde Villacorta&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 18.3 km trail that explores the unique Red and Black Villages of Segovia.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Ayllón area?
A: Yes, many trails around Ayllón are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1155037299/nacimiento-rio-aguisejo-circular-por-grado-del-pico" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nacimiento Río Aguisejo — circular por Grado del Pico&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1156884664/ayllon-san-francisco-convent-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ayllón - San Francisco Convent Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; are both excellent circular options. For a longer, more challenging circular experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1315673019/martin-munoz-de-ayllon-becerril-y-madriguera-circular-desde-villacorta-pueblos-rojos-y-negros-de-segovia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Martín Muñoz de Ayllón, Becerril y Madriguera — circular desde Villacorta&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or points of interest can I expect to see on hikes near Ayllón?
A: Hikers in Ayllón can explore dramatic river gorges like the Hoces del Río Riaza and Hoces del Duratón, which are home to large colonies of griffon vultures. You can also discover ancient beech forests such as the Hayedo de Tejera Negra, and the unique architecture of the 'Red and Black Villages'. Notable highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438613" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrance to Hayedo de Tejera Negra Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4225602" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Risco de la Cruz de Hontanares&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific trails that showcase the 'Red and Black Villages'?
A: Yes, the region is famous for its 'Red and Black Villages'. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1314972163/trail-of-the-red-and-black-villages-i" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Red and Black Villages I&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 11.5 km route connecting these traditional settlements. For a longer exploration, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1315501379/trail-of-the-red-and-black-villages-ii" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Red and Black Villages II&lt;/a&gt; extends for 15.9 km, offering a deeper dive into the region's unique architecture.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Ayllón?
A: The hiking trails around Ayllón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from the impressive river gorges and ancient forests to the charming traditional villages. The variety of routes, suitable for different abilities, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Is it possible to see waterfalls on hikes near Ayllón?
A: While not directly within Ayllón, the region offers access to natural attractions like El Chorro de Navafría, a beautiful waterfall located in the Sierra de Guadarrama, about 80 km away. A path from a nearby recreational park leads to the waterfall, surrounded by a lush Scots pine forest.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for hiking in the Ayllón area?
A: Most hiking trails around Ayllón do not require specific permits. However, for certain protected areas like the Hayedo de Montejo (Montejo Beech Forest), which is part of the broader Sierra de Ayllón, access requires a pass that must be obtained in advance. Always check the specific requirements for the natural park or forest you plan to visit.

Q: What is the best season for hiking around Ayllón?
A: Ayllón offers hiking opportunities throughout the year, but the best season largely depends on your preferences. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors, making them ideal for exploring forests and gorges. Summer can be warm, but riverside trails or higher elevations offer cooler options. Winter hiking is also possible, especially for those seeking a quieter experience, though some higher routes may require appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any places to explore or landmarks near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, beyond the natural beauty, there are several interesting places and landmarks. You can visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4623981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor de Riaza&lt;/a&gt;, the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4225597" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hontanares Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;, or the charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5306654" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Muyo&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features several mountain peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6136008" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón de los Arcos&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6500429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Gordo&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views.

Q: Is it possible to reach hiking trailheads around Ayllón using public transport?
A: While Ayllón itself is accessible by public transport, reaching specific trailheads outside the main town often requires a car. Some of the more remote natural parks and forests may have limited or no direct public transport links. It's advisable to check local bus schedules if you plan to rely on public transport for specific routes.
tips: Ayllón is one of the towns located further north in the province of Segovia and it is important not to miss the walk through its streets and historic buildings!
Old convent that was located close to the town of Ayllón and whose exterior plan can still be seen. It is worth going there and seeing the history of its walls.

faqs.question: Is the Convento de San Francisco circular trail suitable for all fitness levels?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally rated as moderate, it features some easy access paths, making it broadly accessible. However, a good physical condition is recommended for the most comfortable experience, especially if you're not accustomed to hiking.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail is situated in the picturesque Ayllón region, known for its diverse landscapes. You can expect to encounter paths through areas with fruit trees, green meadows, and potentially views of limestone cliffs and valleys, often accompanied by the sounds of a river and local birds.

faqs.question: What historical sites or landmarks will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The most significant feature is the historical San Francisco Convent, traditionally believed to have been founded by Saint Francis of Assisi. You'll also pass through the charming Main Square of Ayllón, offering a glimpse into the town's heritage.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Convento de San Francisco circular trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for this exact trailhead are not provided, but Ayllón is a small town. It's generally advisable to look for public parking areas within the municipality of Ayllón, especially near the town center or the outskirts where the convent is located. From there, you can easily access the start of the circular route.

faqs.question: Is the Convento de San Francisco from Ayllón — circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions mentioned for dogs on this specific trail, it's always best practice to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas like Ayllón or near any private property. Please also ensure you clean up after your pet to maintain the natural environment.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Convento de San Francisco from Ayllón — circular trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The Ayllón region offers pleasant hiking conditions during spring and autumn, with milder temperatures and beautiful natural scenery. Summer can be warm, so an early start is recommended, while winter might bring colder weather, though the trail remains accessible.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, parts of this route overlap with sections of the Camino Natural de la Cañada Real Soriana Occidental and the Ruta del Sureste, offering connections to broader trail networks.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the trail starts and ends in Ayllón, you'll find various amenities within the town, including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options where you can relax and refuel after your hike.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and duration, we recommend bringing comfortable hiking shoes, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a charged phone. Layers of clothing are also advisable to adapt to changing weather conditions.

faqs.question: Can I reach Ayllón by public transport to access the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, Ayllón is accessible by public transport, primarily by bus from larger cities like Madrid or Segovia. Check local bus schedules for the most up-to-date information on routes and timings to Ayllón bus station, from where the trail is easily reachable.
